title: colea ramiflora
intro: Colea ramiflora, also known as the mountain ebony, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Bignoniaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Colea ramiflora include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antipyretic. Herbal preparations of Colea ramiflora, often in the form of infusions or decoctions, are commonly used in traditional African medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
